jobs.title: Jobs register & change-tracking traceability
jobs.description: Add a SpecPad-owned jobs register (jobs.json) with an open/closed lifecycle, the active-job governance rule, the editor Jobs view and active-job picker, and the skill git plumbing so every spec change traces to a job. Baselined at v1.1.

jobs.title: Onboarding: specpad init
jobs.description: One-command setup that scaffolds docs/specpad/, installs the pre-push hook, wires the SpecPad working-loop directive into the project CLAUDE.md, and generates the launcher — so a new user is fully set up on install with no manual configuration. Idempotent and non-destructive.

jobs.title: Baseline generator (draft a spec from existing code)
jobs.description: An agent procedure that bootstraps a draft v1 SRS/VTP from an existing codebase — surveying modules, public behavior, and existing tests — so SpecPad can be adopted on a project that did not start with it. Output is a draft for human ratification, mapped to existing tests, governance-clean.

jobs.title: Requirement audit (reconcile spec with code)
jobs.description: An agent procedure that reconciles the existing SRS/VTP against the whole codebase, reporting drift both directions — code behavior with no requirement (missing), requirements whose behavior is gone/changed (stale), and requirements/test references with no covering test (coverage). Reports ratifiable proposals; applies nothing destructive. The commit-time audit is its staged-diff form.

jobs.title: Spec reconciliation (apply requirement-audit findings)
jobs.description: Apply the ratified findings from the first requirement audit: add the missing recent-projects requirement (EDS-12) mapped to its existing tests, correct the stale ValidationPanel test reference (TEST-22 -> StatusBar), and close two of the four not_tested coverage gaps (TEST-9 id-immutability, TEST-59 records-only) with real tests.

jobs.title: Jobs view as release notes
jobs.description: Reframe the editor Jobs tab as release notes: group jobs by major version (unversioned under Unreleased), then by type into Features and Bugfixes, with each job's description beneath its title. Clicking a job opens a separate detail view. Adds the job record type and version fields and moves Jobs to the leftmost tab.

jobs.title: Closed-job change cache, owner, derived version, in-progress vs released views
jobs.description: On close, cache a job's before/after spec snapshots (lockfile-class) so the browser editor can show its SRS/VTP changes; add an owner set from git at creation; derive a job's version from the release tag containing its commits; split the Jobs tab into In progress (open) and Released (closed) views.

jobs.title: Source-code traceability (job to commits to code)
jobs.description: Cache each closed job's commit list (commits.json, derived from the Job: trailer) so the editor shows a job's commits alongside its SRS/VTP changes, and document the skill's source-traceability export (per-job commits + code diffs from git) that feeds the eQMS evidence package.

jobs.title: Architecture spec (arc42 + C4) and Architecture view
jobs.description: Add an optional architecture spec — an arc42 software architecture document (markdown) plus a C4 model in Structurizr DSL — tracked under docs/specpad/, job/release coupled (no requirement-level trace matrix). New editor Architecture view renders the arc42 doc and presents the C4 DSL. Dogfooded with SpecPad's own SAD.
